title: The arithmetic guard is a disguised hardcoded unlock that always passes after a preset date
reason: This is the mechanism that makes F-001 exploitable, but it does not create distinct protocol harm beyond the permissionless drain already captured there.

title: Using tx.origin as the beneficiary misroutes withdrawals and enables phishing-style abuse
reason: `tx.origin` is poor practice, but here it does not add a separate exploitable loss beyond F-001 because the function is already permissionless and always pays the top-level caller.
